attacking_style: Explosive accelerations in final 3-6km of high-altitude climbs (2000m+), often solo. Lacks punch on short explosive gradients but dominates sustained irregular-gradient finishes above 2000m altitude.
team_tactics: Astana typically commits fully to his leadership with designated support, but positioning failures and mechanicals suggest organizational chaos. Team sets tempo on final climbs but Lopez often isolated when decisive moves happen.
terrain_preference: Thrives on high-altitude (2000m+) irregular-gradient climbs with sustained final 5-6km. Exposed badly on short explosive walls, gravel sections, and when pace surges suddenly after steady tempo.
vulnerability: Collapses spectacularly when explosive accelerations hit after tempo riding; poor pacing discipline leads to premature energy expenditure; struggles in week three with multiple instances of complete form breakdown including abandonment.
key_patterns: High-altitude specialist: three stage wins at 2000m+ in 2017, consistently drops rivals above 2000m elevation
Explosive gradient weakness: dropped on Los Machucos, struggled with VALVERDE/PADUN on punchy finishes (4+ observations)
Week three vulnerability: multiple collapses, abandonment in 2021, described as 'more disappointing than Quintana' late-race
Mechanicals and positioning chaos: five mechanicals/crashes at 2019 Giro, multiple desperate chases by Astana
Inconsistent form pattern: bridged for bonuses after earlier struggles, 'legs waking up' comments indicate volatility
Tactically patient when strong: waited for Roglic to mark Bernal before attacking for 2021 win
form_trajectory: negative: 2022 Vuelta showed continued pattern of incremental time losses, poor explosive gradient adaptation, and defensive rather than opportunistic racing. Bridging for bonus seconds suggests damage control rather than genuine GC threat.
